title: plantago sempervirens
intro: Plantago sempervirens, also known as the sea plantain or coastal plantain, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is commonly found in coastal areas and salt marshes. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties that can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. The health benefits of Plantago sempervirens include its ability to support respiratory health, reduce coughs and colds, and help to manage digestive issues such as diarrhea and irritable bowel syndrome. Herbal preparations of Plantago sempervirens include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
